master_row.title: Azimuthal Quantum Communication
master_row.canonical_statement: Classical communication is bounded by the speed of light: no signal travels faster than $c$. Quantum entanglement appears to transcend this — entangled particles exhibit correlations across arbitrary distances instantaneously — but the no communication theorem forbids using entanglement alone to transmit information faster than light. The Azimuthal Quantum Communication system, developed across the corpus, works within these constraints while exploiting the topological structure of the TZP framework to achieve communication architectures impossible in ordinary quantum information theory. It uses the teleportation manifold, the atemporal channel structure, and the unified entanglement meta theorem to build a communication system organised around the DAANSphere's 153,600 node addressing — the "Azimuthal" system that gives the volume its name.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a series / summation relating Q.
master_row.dictionary: Classical communication is bounded by the speed of light: no signal travels faster than c.
master_row.encyclopedia: network. --- ## Part Four: The Unified Entanglement Meta-Theorem ### The Master Result The corpus develops a unified entanglement meta-theorem (ID 0522) that ties the communication system together. The meta-theorem states, in essence, that **all the entanglement resources in the Azimuthal system are unified by a single conserved quantity — the total topological charge** (ID 025). The meta-theorem has the structure: for any configuration of entangled resources on the DAANSphere, the sum of the entanglement entropies, weighted by the winding numbers, equals the total topological charge: This means entanglement cannot be created or destroyed arbitrarily — it can only be moved, swapped, and reconfigured, subject to the conservation of total topological charge. **Entanglement, in the Azimuthal system, is a conserved topological quantity** — the communication-theoretic expression of the conser
